request.question:
Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.
In plain English: can we deliver chemotherapy more precisely to cancer cells by attaching it to a targeting molecule?

hypothesis.intervention: conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles
hypothesis.threshold: at least 3-fold higher compared to free doxorubicin; at least 40% reduction in cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific, providing a complete hypothesis.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: doxorubicin folate PLGA nanoparticles HeLa cells drug accumulation
hypothesis.original_hypothesis:
Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.
In plain English: can we deliver chemotherapy more precisely to cancer cells by attaching it to a targeting molecule?

references.title: Hybrid PEGylated chitosan/PLGA nanoparticles designed as pH-responsive vehicles to promote intracellular drug delivery and cancer chemotherapy.
references.relevance_note: This paper discusses PLGA nanoparticles for drug delivery, providing insights into a similar targeted drug delivery methodology that could inform the experimental design for HeLa cells.

references.title: Combinatorial delivery of superparamagnetic iron oxide nanoparticles (γFe2O3) and doxorubicin using folate conjugated redox sensitive multiblock polymeric nanocarriers for enhancing the chemotherapeutic efficacy in cancer cells.
references.relevance_note: This paper describes folate-conjugated nanoparticle systems used in drug delivery, which is relevant as it explores a similar biochemical targeting strategy that aligns with the proposed intervention.

literature_qc.explanation: There are studies that involve folate-functionalized nanoparticles for drug delivery (as seen in paper [5]), which relates to the intervention dimension. While specific details about HeLa cells were not consistently reported, the focus on PLGA nanoparticles is present in works like [3] and [4], thereby covering the biological system to some extent. However, none of these papers address intracellular drug accumulation specifically; they cover related experimental types and methodologies.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

plan.objective: To evaluate the intracellular drug accumulation of doxorubicin conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles in HeLa cells compared to free doxorubicin.
plan.experimental_design: This experiment will involve synthesizing folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles that encapsulate doxorubicin. The intracellular drug accumulation will be quantified in HeLa cells and compared to cells treated with free doxorubicin. Additionally, cytotoxicity will be assessed in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells to evaluate specificity.
controls: Free doxorubicin at a concentration of 100 µM in culture medium.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Dissolve 100 mg of PLGA (Resomer RG 503 H, Evonik) in 10 mL of dichloromethane at room temperature to create a polymer solution.
Expected outcome: Homogeneous PLGA solution is achieved.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Mix 1 mL of doxorubicin hydrochloride at 1 mg/mL with the PLGA solution and stir for 1 h in the dark at room temperature.
Expected outcome: Uniform mixture of doxorubicin and PLGA is formed.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 5 min | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Add 2 mL of 1% w/v polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) solution to the mixture and vortex for 5 min to create an emulsion.
Expected outcome: Stable emulsion is formed.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Remove the dichloromethane under reduced pressure using a rotary evaporator at 40°C until the emulsion is consistent.
Expected outcome: Dry nanoparticles are formed.

DAY 1 | 20 min | Nanoparticle Centrifugation
Centrifuge the dried nanoparticles at 10,000 × g for 20 min at 4°C to remove excess PVA.
Expected outcome: Nanoparticles precipitate as a pellet.

DAY 1 | 15 min | Nanoparticle Resuspension
Resuspend the pellet in 5 mL of sterile PBS for storage and use in cell culture experiments.
Expected outcome: Nanoparticles are homogeneously suspended in PBS.

DAY 1 | 24 h | Cell Culture
Plate 1 × 10^5 HeLa cells in a 6-well plate and allow them to adhere overnight in DMEM supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum at 37°C and 5% CO2.
Expected outcome: Cells are adherent and ready for treatment.

DAY 2 | 4 h | Treatment Administration
Treat HeLa cells with either 100 µM doxorubicin, 100 µM folate-PLGA-doxorubicin nanoparticles, or control for 4 h at 37°C.
Expected outcome: Incubation allows cellular uptake of doxorubicin variants.

DAY 2 | 2 h | Cytotoxicity Assay
Assess cell viability using MTT assay by adding 100 µL of MTT solution (5 mg/mL in PBS) to each well and incubating for 2 h at 37°C.
Expected outcome: MTT conversion is quantified to measure cell viability.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Quantify the intracellular doxorubicin accumulation using fluorescence spectroscopy at excitation/emission of 485/590 nm and calculate fold change.
Expected outcome: Intracellular drug accumulation is quantified and compared across treatments.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Folate receptors are overexpressed on HeLa cells compared to HEK293 cells.
Conjugation of doxorubicin to PLGA nanoparticles increases cellular uptake via folate receptor-mediated endocytosis.
Cytotoxicity measurements will reflect the viability of cells post-treatment.
decision_criteria: Proceed if intracellular doxorubicin accumulation in HeLa cells is at least 3-fold higher than that of free doxorubicin.
Proceed if cytotoxicity in HEK293 cells treated with folate-PLGA-doxorubicin nanoparticles is at least 40% lower than that of free doxorubicin.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same lot number of PLGA and doxorubicin for all experiments.
Standardize incubation times for all cell types.
Ensure that the same cell passage number is used for all treatments.

staffing.cro_delegation_recommendation: Consider CRO delegation for 'Day 3: Data Analysis and Results Write-up' due to the complexity of data analysis and the need for specialized bioinformatics expertise.
staffing.staffing_notes: The experiment involves sequential tasks that require a Research Associate for nanoparticle synthesis and cell culture, while a Bioinformatician is needed for data analysis. The Research Associate can handle multiple tasks but cannot independently perform data analysis. Biosafety training is required for handling HeLa cells.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 3-fold higher compared to free doxorubicin; at least 40% reduction in cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
